editableNewDialogue.text: Can you help me finish a casual message to a friend? I want it to sound warm but not super formal.
Sure—share what you have so far (or the vibe you want), and I’ll help you finish it. Do you want it to be a little funny, more heartfelt, or a mix?
Let’s do a mix: caring + a tiny joke, but nothing cheesy.

Got it. Here are two options you can copy/paste:

Option 1:
"Hey! Just wanted to say I’m really grateful for you. Thanks for always being in my corner—life’s honestly easier (and way funnier) with you around. If you ever need anything, I’ve got you… even if it’s just to hype you up or send bad memes." 

Option 2:
"Hi! Random reminder that you mean a lot to me. I’m really lucky we’re friends. Also, I’m still waiting for the day we finally act like responsible adults, but until then—I’m glad I get to do life with you."
Nice. Can you make it a bit shorter?
“Hey—just a quick reminder that I really appreciate you. Thanks for always being there for me. Life’s better with you in it (even when we’re a mess).”
Unrelated: what’s a fun fact I can add at the end?

A light, not-too-random one: “Fun fact: octopuses have three hearts.”

Or a quick space one: “Fun fact: a day on Venus is longer than its year.”
can you finish that message for Sarina? keep it casual—she’ll roast me if it sounds like an email lol

Hey Sarina,

I just wanted to take a moment to let you know how much I appreciate your friendship. You always know how to make me laugh and brighten my day, and your company is always the highlight of my day.

Thanks for being my go-to person for everything—the random rants, the dumb jokes, and the serious talks too. I’m really lucky to have you in my life.

Love you lots (not in a weird way),

(Your Name)
btw, quick correction about those love-letter prompts earlier—Ayda isn’t my girlfriend 😅 i just needed a sample letter with a Persian name for a class.

Got it—so Ayda isn’t your girlfriend, it was just a placeholder name for an example. If you tell me the vibe (cute, funny, super romantic) and the real name (or if you want it anonymous), I can rewrite it.
yeah exactly. no real person—just an example.
Perfect—then we’ll keep it generic and just treat “Ayda” as a fictional name for the sample.
